assertions.title: Multiple choice shows 4 options (A/B/C/D) as clickable buttons

steps.observed: Drill filter page with Start Drill button displayed
First question was code-tracing (no options), skipped to get to multiple-choice
Second question displayed with 4 options: A JL, B JG, C JE, D JB
Options rendered as <button> elements: 'A JL', 'B JG', 'C JE', 'D JB'

assertions.title: Submit button becomes enabled after selecting answer

steps.action: Navigate to a multiple-choice drill question
steps.observed: Submit Answer button showed [disabled] before selection
After clicking 'A', Submit Answer button no longer showed [disabled]

assertions.title: Immediate feedback shows correct/incorrect after submit

steps.action: Select answer and click Submit Answer
steps.observed: Feedback displayed: 'Incorrect. The correct answer was: CA'
Feedback clearly showed 'Incorrect' and correct answer was displayed

assertions.title: Explanation with remediation links shown after feedback

steps.action: Submit answer and wait for feedback
steps.observed: Explanation section showed: 'Binary 11001010 groups into 1100 (12 = C) and 1010 (10 = A), giving CA in hexadecimal.'
Review Material section had links: 'Introduction to number systems' and 'Hexadecimal representation'

assertions.title: End-of-drill summary shows total correct, incorrect, skipped, accuracy

steps.action: Complete or skip all drill questions
steps.observed: Drill completed with Results Summary page
Summary displayed: 10 Total Questions, 0 Correct, 3 Incorrect, 7 Skipped, 0% Overall Accuracy, 2m 4s Time Taken

steps.expected: Overview, By Concept, Misconceptions, Remediation tabs
steps.observed: Summary had: Overview, By Concept, Misconceptions, Remediation buttons; Export JSON, Share, Try Again buttons

frictions.description: First question in drill was code-tracing type (no multiple choice options), required skipping to reach a multiple-choice question
frictions.resolution: Skipped first question to reach a question with 4 A/B/C/D options

frictions.description: agent-browser click command with @e12 format failed; had to use 'find text' approach for clicking
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser find text 'A' click --exact for clicking answer options

summary: Tested 5 assertions (P3-DRILL-003, P3-DRILL-004, P3-DRILL-005, P3-DRILL-006, P3-DRILL-010): All passed. Multiple choice options render as button elements (not span), submit enables after selection, immediate feedback shows correct/incorrect with explanation and remediation links, and end-of-drill summary displays all statistics (correct/incorrect/skipped/accuracy/time).
